extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNISSEN Mike retained yellow as Jumbo-Visma won team time trial by 20 seconds over Ineos, who started first and set benchmark time of 29:18.
tactical_patterns: Ineos starting first as defending champions lost psychological advantage, allowing rivals to chase known target time throughout parcours.
Quick-Step/Deceuninck lost stage by 0.75 seconds, showing fourth-man discipline at line determines TTT outcomes more than raw speed.
Tony Martin visibly struggling to hold wheels in final km suggests Jumbo's roster depth exceeded individual star power approach.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Ineos designated him to cross line first for potential yellow jersey, revealing GC hierarchy over Bernal already established
rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Mike: positive: unexpectedly took hard pull with 2km remaining while in yellow, risking jersey loss but demonstrating team-first mentality
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: sprinting to rejoin wheels in closing kilometer exposes declining TTT form despite reputation as time trial specialist
